PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Apr-16-2019 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Apr-09-2019 Continued To May-07-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Wrobel for Petitioner.

Mr. Wrobel stated that this was a State registered domestic partnership, that it is recorded and that he can supplement the record. Mr. Wrobel also stated that item two was marked in error and that the Decedent was an only child. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to May 7, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Mar-05-2019 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Mar-05-2019 Continued To Apr-16-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ronald Berman appeared by CourtCall for Danny Newland (Petitioner).
David Herman (Interested Party/Domestic Partner Of Jeffrey Beckett) appeared in pro per.

The Court stated before it are two petitions for letters of administration, that a withdrawal by Petitioner Derek Vanacore was filed, that the remaining petition before the Court is Danny Newland's petition, and that there are objections to that petition. Mr. Herman stated that he did not know about this petition and only received information from the unclaimed property division. The Court inquired whether Mr. Herman opposed the petition. Mr. Herman stated that he had concerns with the middle initial in the name, that he had paperwork with him in court today to address the issue, that there was no person named Jeffrey W. Beckett that died at that address, but that there was a Jeffrey N. Beckett at that address that died on January 6, 2018. The Court inquired a second time as to whether or not Mr. Herman objected. Mr. Herman stated that he was objecting to the petition. The Court stated that it would continue the matter because Mr. Herman is a potential objecter, that it would not get to the merits today, that it strongly urged Mr. Herman to hire an attorney and file a written objection, and that Mr. Herman should talk to the attorney about the significance, if any, with regard to the documentation that he'd like to present to the Court. The Court continued the matter to April 16, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on the filing of objections and/or a competing petition. The objections and/or competing petition is to be on file by April 10, 2019. The Court admonished Mr. Herman about the need to properly file and serve any documents for the Court. The Court also stated that there are still some heirship issues and that this would give counsel additional time to look at the heirs.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy K. Chan. Matter Not Reported.
